class.name: HEGoHeightfieldInputNode
class.brief_description: Builds Houdini HeightField input layers from Godot images.
class.description:
This node creates or recreates a HeightField input network and writes voxel data for [code]height[/code], [code]mask[/code], and any additional named layers.
Each layer entry in the input dictionary is expected to be a dictionary with optional [code]image[/code] ([Image]) and [code]attrs[/code] ([Dictionary]) keys. The first valid image determines resolution. Missing [code]height[/code] or [code]mask[/code] layers are auto-generated as zero-filled layers.
Layer names are normalized to lowercase and trimmed before processing.
Calling [method set_layers] rebuilds the internal heightfield input node network for the new layer payload.

method.name: instantiate
method.description:
Creates a Houdini HeightField input network for this wrapper.
If the node is already instantiated, the call returns immediately.
The method requires an active Houdini session. On success, it stores the main node id plus internal height, mask, and merge node ids, then registers this wrapper with the session manager.
On failure, all stored ids are reset to invalid values and an error is logged.

method.name: set_layers
method.description:
Uploads all supplied layers into a newly created HeightField input node.
[code]voxel_size[/code] must be greater than [code]0[/code] and controls XY world scale. [code]height_scale[/code] must be greater than [code]0[/code] and is applied only to the [code]height[/code] layer values.
Expected [code]layers[/code] structure: [codeblock] { "height": { "image": Image, # optional but usually provided "attrs": { # optional primitive attributes "name": Variant } }, "mask": { "image": Image, "attrs": {} }, "water": { "image": Image, "attrs": { "hego_material": "Mud", "hego_lod": 1 } } } [/codeblock]
How entries are interpreted: - Top-level keys are layer names (for example [code]height[/code], [code]mask[/code], [code]water[/code]). - Each top-level value should be a [Dictionary]. Non-dictionary entries are skipped. - [code]image[/code] should be an [Image]. If present, it is sampled to voxel values. - [code]attrs[/code] is optional primitive metadata written onto that layer volume.
Resolution and defaults: - The first valid [code]image[/code] found across all layers sets the heightfield resolution. - If no valid image exists in any layer, the call logs an error and returns without writing layers. - If [code]height[/code] and/or [code]mask[/code] are missing, they are created as zero-filled layers. - Extra layers beyond [code]height[/code] and [code]mask[/code] are created as additional heightfield volume nodes.
Notes on values: - [code]height_scale[/code] affects only the [code]height[/code] layer. - Layer images are converted or resized as needed to match the final resolution. - Primitive [code]attrs[/code] values are expected to be [code]bool[/code], [code]int[/code], [code]float[/code], [code]String[/code], or [code]Vector3[/code].
Failure behavior: - If [code]voxel_size[/code] or [code]height_scale[/code] is less than or equal to [code]0[/code], the call is rejected. - If no active Houdini session exists, layer upload cannot proceed. - Any HAPI node creation or write failure logs an error and aborts the current layer build.
